steps.description: Rust integration test for Lock flow
steps.command: cd src/api/api && SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo test --test sequence_e2e_test seq1_lock -- --nocapture
steps.expected: All assertions in seq1_lock pass: Lock Request accepted by L3, Dilithium signature verified, nonce and expiry validated, SR_0 computed via SHA3-256("QS_LOCK_V1" || chain_id || asset || amount || dest_addr || expiry || nonce || pk_dilithium), lock_id issued, DB row created, and L1 lockWithSR0 transaction submitted. Test process exits 0 with no FAIL lines in stdout.

steps.description: Playwright integration spec for Consumer Lock
steps.command: cd src/frontend/web && npx playwright test e2e/integration/lock.integration.spec.ts --reporter=json
steps.expected: All Playwright test cases in lock.integration.spec.ts pass: UI Lock Request submission transitions to a success state, a corresponding locks DB row is observable, and no test case reports status failed or timedOut in the JSON reporter output. Exit code is 0.

steps.description: Verify lock row was created during this run window, pk_dilithium populated, and L1 submission was attempted
steps.command: psql "$DATABASE_URL" -t -A -c "SELECT lock_id, status, length(pk_dilithium), l1_tx_hash IS NOT NULL AS l1_submitted FROM locks WHERE created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '5 minutes' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;"
steps.expected: Exactly one row is returned from the last 5-minute window. The status column is one of {pending, locked, confirmed}. The length(pk_dilithium) column is exactly 1952 (Dilithium public key byte length). The l1_submitted column is t when the L1 Vault is reachable, confirming an L1 transaction hash was recorded. Any deviation — zero rows, wrong pk length, wrong status, or l1_submitted=f — is a hard failure.

steps.description: Verify the lock created in this run is anchored on L1 (receipt status==0x1)
steps.command:
bash -c '
set -eo pipefail
LOCK_ID=$(psql "$DATABASE_URL" -t -A -c "SELECT lock_id FROM locks WHERE created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '"'"'5 minutes'"'"' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;" | tr -d " ")
TX_HASH=$(psql "$DATABASE_URL" -t -A -c "SELECT l1_tx_hash FROM locks WHERE lock_id = '"'"'$LOCK_ID'"'"';" | tr -d " ")
if [ -z "$TX_HASH" ] || [ "$TX_HASH" = "" ]; then
  echo "FAIL: lock $LOCK_ID has no l1_tx_hash (L1 anchoring did not occur in this run; QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY may be unset)"
  echo "Total locked snapshot for context (should be unchanged from prior runs):"
  cast call 0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260 "totalLocked()(uint256)" --rpc-url "$L1_RPC_URL" || true
  exit 1
fi
echo "checking L1 receipt for tx=$TX_HASH (lock=$LOCK_ID)"
RECEIPT_STATUS=$(cast receipt "$TX_HASH" --rpc-url "$L1_RPC_URL" --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r ".status // \"missing\"" || echo "missing")
echo "receipt status: $RECEIPT_STATUS"
case "$RECEIPT_STATUS" in
  0x1|1) echo "PASS: lock anchored on L1"; exit 0 ;;
  0x0|0) echo "FAIL: tx reverted on L1"; exit 1 ;;
  missing) echo "FAIL: tx not found on L1 (tx submitted but never mined, or wrong RPC)"; exit 1 ;;
  *) echo "FAIL: unexpected receipt status $RECEIPT_STATUS"; exit 1 ;;
esac
'
steps.expected: PASS: lock anchored on L1 — the most recent locks row within the 5-minute window has a non-empty l1_tx_hash, and cast receipt for that hash against L1_RPC_URL returns status==0x1. A reverted transaction (0x0), a missing transaction, or an absent l1_tx_hash are each hard failures. The Locked event must have been emitted by the L1 Vault at address 0x6F889C00a5e674ab0b9403AfBa0fBEbe30511c67 as specified in the spec, and totalLocked on the Vault must be non-zero.

acceptance_criteria: Backend integration test seq1_lock exits 0 with all assertions passing: Dilithium signature verified, nonce checked, expiry checked, SR_0 computed, lock_id issued, DB row created, L1 lockWithSR0 submitted
Playwright lock.integration.spec.ts exits 0 with all test cases in status passed in the JSON reporter output
A locks row exists with created_at within the last 5 minutes, status in {pending, locked, confirmed}, length(pk_dilithium) == 1952, and l1_tx_hash IS NOT NULL
The l1_tx_hash stored in the locks row resolves to a mined transaction receipt with status == 0x1 on L1 (Sepolia)
The L1 Vault at 0x6F889C00a5e674ab0b9403AfBa0fBEbe30511c67 emitted a Locked event for the lock_id created in this run
totalLocked() on the L1 Vault is non-zero after the run
No hardcoded private key, address substitution, or silent fallback is present in any command trace or log output

layer_results.command: psql "$DATABASE_URL" -t -A -c "SELECT lock_id, status, length(pk_dilithium), l1_tx_hash IS NOT NULL AS l1_submitted FROM locks WHERE created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '5 minutes' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;"

bash -c '
set -eo pipefail
LOCK_ID=$(psql "$DATABASE_URL" -t -A -c "SELECT lock_id FROM locks WHERE created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '"'"'5 minutes'"'"' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;" | tr -d " ")
TX_HASH=$(psql "$DATABASE_URL" -t -A -c "SELECT l1_tx_hash FROM locks WHERE lock_id = '"'"'$LOCK_ID'"'"';" | tr -d " ")
if [ -z "$TX_HASH" ] || [ "$TX_HASH" = "" ]; then
  echo "FAIL: lock $LOCK_ID has no l1_tx_hash (L1 anchoring did not occur in this run; QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY may be unset)"
  echo "Total locked snapshot for context (should be unchanged from prior runs):"
  cast call 0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260 "totalLocked()(uint256)" --rpc-url "$L1_RPC_URL" || true
  exit 1
fi
echo "checking L1 receipt for tx=$TX_HASH (lock=$LOCK_ID)"
RECEIPT_STATUS=$(cast receipt "$TX_HASH" --rpc-url "$L1_RPC_URL" --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r ".status // \"missing\"" || echo "missing")
echo "receipt status: $RECEIPT_STATUS"
case "$RECEIPT_STATUS" in
  0x1|1) echo "PASS: lock anchored on L1"; exit 0 ;;
  0x0|0) echo "FAIL: tx reverted on L1"; exit 1 ;;
  missing) echo "FAIL: tx not found on L1 (tx submitted but never mined, or wrong RPC)"; exit 1 ;;
  *) echo "FAIL: unexpected receipt status $RECEIPT_STATUS"; exit 1 ;;
esac
'

Lock integration tests confirm: POST /v1/lock endpoint functional, database persistence working, Explorer integration operational, validation rules enforced (nonce reuse, expiration, required fields)

status: pending — valid state (in {pending, locked, confirmed})
length(pk_dilithium): 1952 bytes — correct Dilithium public key length
l1_submitted (l1_tx_hash IS NOT NULL): false — L1 Vault unreachable or transaction not yet recorded

issues.title: L1 transaction not submitted
issues.detail: The l1_submitted column is false, indicating either the L1 Vault is unreachable or no L1 transaction hash has been recorded yet. The expected post-condition requires l1_submitted=true when L1 Vault is reachable.

observations: Script exited with code 1 (failure)
L1 anchoring did not occur: no l1_tx_hash found in locks table for the most recent lock (0x22b24489b28cffd1fa685205cd4652a6416ba550c9bb8e498602e659c585c53f)
Root cause indicator: QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY may be unset, preventing transaction submission to L1
L1Vault totalLocked() call succeeded with result 120000000000000000 wei (1.2e17), indicating the vault contract is reachable and functional
The test expected a lock to be anchored on L1 with a valid l1_tx_hash and receipt status 0x1, but the anchor transaction was never submitted

issues.title: L1 anchoring disabled or misconfigured
issues.detail: The most recent lock has no l1_tx_hash, indicating the Consumer Lock did not submit a transaction to L1Vault. The script suggests QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY may be unset, which would prevent transaction signing and submission.

issues.title: L1Vault is reachable and responsive
issues.detail: cast call to 0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260 totalLocked() returned 120000000000000000 wei. The L1 contract and RPC are functioning.

quality_findings.title: L1 anchoring silently skipped — lock persists as 'pending' with no on-chain record
quality_findings.detail: The Consumer Lock sequence completed successfully at every off-chain layer (backend 4/4, frontend 40/40, DB row written) but the lock was never submitted to L1Vault. The DB row shows l1_submitted=false and status='pending'; the L1 layer confirms no l1_tx_hash exists. This is a cross-layer silent failure: the API returned 200, the DB row looks healthy, and the frontend declared success — yet the canonical on-chain state was never updated. A lock that never reaches L1 is indistinguishable from a successful lock to the consumer, but provides no actual cryptographic commitment on the chain. The vault's totalLocked() value (120000000000000000 wei) is unchanged, confirming no ETH moved. The L1 script itself notes 'QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY may be unset' as the probable cause, meaning the background task responsible for L1 submission either silently swallowed the signing error (matching known silent-failure pattern #5: Ok(()) swallowing background task errors) or was never started. This is precisely the 'best-effort L1 call that warns-and-continues' anti-pattern (silent-failure pattern #13) applied to the anchoring path.
evidence: L1 layer stdout: 'FAIL: lock 0x22b24489b28cffd1fa685205cd4652a6416ba550c9bb8e498602e659c585c53f has no l1_tx_hash (L1 anchoring did not occur in this run; QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY may be unset)'
DB layer stdout: '0x22b24489b28cffd1fa685205cd4652a6416ba550c9bb8e498602e659c585c53f|pending|1952|f' — l1_submitted column is 'f' (false)
L1 layer stdout: 'Total locked snapshot for context (should be unchanged from prior runs): 120000000000000000 [1.2e17]' — vault balance did not move
Backend stdout: '"status": "pending"' in lock response — status was never advanced to 'locked' or 'confirmed' by an L1 event
Frontend test passed: '[Lock Persisted] lock_id=0x4a0f8d3b..., status=pending' — frontend declared persistence success despite no L1 submission
L1 layer exit_code=1 while backend exit_code=0 and frontend exit_code=0 — cross-layer status divergence

quality_findings.title: QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY absence does not hard-fail the lock request — consumer receives false-positive 200
quality_findings.detail: When QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY is absent or invalid, the lock endpoint still returns HTTP 200 with a lock_id and SR_0, giving the consumer no indication that the lock will never be anchored on L1. The correct behaviour is either (a) reject the lock request at submission time with a 503/500 if the signing key is unavailable, or (b) ensure the background anchor task hard-fails and rolls back / marks the DB row in an error state that the consumer can observe. Instead, the row sits in 'pending' indefinitely with no error signal. This is a variant of the 'Ok(()) swallowing background task errors' pattern and directly violates the fail-hard requirement for L1 submission paths (silent-failure pattern #13).
evidence: L1 stdout: 'QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY may be unset' — signing key availability is not a hard precondition for accepting the request
DB stdout: 'status=pending' with 'l1_tx_hash IS NOT NULL = f' — row created without any error flag despite L1 submission failure
Backend exit_code=0, frontend exit_code=0 — no error surfaced to callers despite the anchor never being submitted
L1 exit_code=1 — the only signal of failure is in a separate monitoring script, not in the API response or DB state

quality_findings.title: Frontend and backend tests do not assert l1_tx_hash presence — gap allows silent L1 anchoring failures to go undetected
quality_findings.detail: All 40 frontend integration tests and all 4 backend e2e tests passed, yet the fundamental postcondition of the Consumer Lock sequence (a transaction hash anchoring the lock on L1) was not verified by any of them. The frontend test '[Lock Persisted] status=pending' treats 'pending' as a terminal success state. The backend test 'test_lock_creates_successfully' likewise does not poll for the lock to advance beyond 'pending' or assert a non-null l1_tx_hash. This means the entire test suite provides a false green signal whenever the L1 submission path is broken. Tests should assert that within a reasonable timeout the lock status advances to 'locked'/'confirmed' and l1_tx_hash is non-null.
evidence: Frontend stdout: '[Lock Persisted] lock_id=0x4a0f8d3b628bb60bb80c213f25f76c4e9bbc2b5a409f989648a65e5552cbf4f8, status=pending' — test asserts only on 'pending', not on eventual L1 confirmation
Backend stdout: '"status": "pending"' — backend test accepts pending as pass condition
DB stdout: 'l1_submitted=f' — no test in backend or frontend layer checked this column
frontend stats: 'expected: 40, unexpected: 0' — full green suite despite no L1 anchor

fixer_proposals.title: Hard-fail lock submission when QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY is absent or L1 signing is unavailable
fixer_proposals.rationale: Addresses the critical finding (bug-hunter: 'L1 anchoring silently skipped') and high finding ('QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY absence does not hard-fail the lock request'). The root cause is that the background L1 anchoring task swallows the signing error (silent-failure patterns #5 and #13) and the lock endpoint returns HTTP 200 regardless. The minimum correct fix is: (1) at startup, validate that QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY is present and parseable; (2) in the lock handler, check L1 signer availability before writing the DB row and return HTTP 503 if unavailable; (3) in the background anchor task, propagate errors to the DB row (set status='error', record error_detail) rather than returning Ok(()). Because the exact file paths and line numbers are not available from the evidence, unified_diff is left empty and the full change description is given here for manual implementation. Files likely involved: src/api/api/src/routes/locks.rs (lock handler), src/api/api/src/tasks/l1_anchor.rs or equivalent (background anchor task), src/api/api/src/main.rs or config.rs (startup validation).

fixer_proposals.title: Investigation required — locate and fix the Ok(()) swallow in the L1 anchor background task
fixer_proposals.rationale: Addresses bug-hunter critical finding. The evidence shows the background task responsible for submitting to L1Vault completed without error from the API's perspective (backend exit_code=0, HTTP 200 returned) while the L1 submission never occurred. The pattern matches 'Ok(()) swallowing background task errors'. Steps to investigate: (1) Search src/api/api/src/ for spawned tokio tasks or background futures that call the L1 signer/submitter — look for patterns like `tokio::spawn(async move { ... })` where the JoinHandle is dropped or errors are logged-and-continued. (2) Find where the wallet/signer is constructed from QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY; confirm whether a missing env var causes a silent None or an error that is caught and discarded. (3) Confirm the DB update path: if L1 submission returns Err, does the code update the locks row to status='error' or does it log a warning and return Ok(())? (4) Assert that any Err from the anchor task is written to locks.error_detail and locks.status='error' so the consumer can observe the failure.

fixer_proposals.title: Add startup guard that panics if QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY is missing or unparseable
fixer_proposals.rationale: Addresses the high finding 'QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY absence does not hard-fail the lock request'. A missing signing key is a misconfiguration that makes the entire service non-functional for its primary purpose; the service should refuse to start rather than accept requests it cannot fulfill. The fix is a startup validation block in main.rs (or the config loader) that calls std::env::var("QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY").expect("QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY must be set") and additionally attempts to parse it as a valid private key, returning a descriptive error if parsing fails. Because exact line numbers are unavailable, unified_diff is left empty.

fixer_proposals.title: Add backend e2e assertion that lock advances beyond 'pending' and l1_tx_hash is non-null
fixer_proposals.rationale: Addresses the medium finding 'Frontend and backend tests do not assert l1_tx_hash presence'. The existing backend test accepts status='pending' as a terminal success condition, providing a false green signal when L1 anchoring is broken. The fix adds a polling loop (max ~30s, 1s intervals) after the lock creation call that re-fetches the lock by lock_id and asserts: (a) status is 'locked' or 'confirmed', not 'pending' or 'error'; (b) l1_tx_hash is Some(_) and non-empty. This test will correctly fail in CI whenever QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY is unset or L1 submission is broken, eliminating the false-green signal. Because exact file paths and line numbers are not available, unified_diff is left empty.

fixer_proposals.title: Add frontend integration test assertion that lock status advances to 'locked'/'confirmed' with non-null l1_tx_hash
fixer_proposals.rationale: Addresses the medium finding 'Frontend and backend tests do not assert l1_tx_hash presence'. The frontend test '[Lock Persisted]' currently treats status='pending' as success. The fix updates the relevant test in src/frontend/web (likely a Playwright or Jest integration test) to poll the lock status endpoint after creation and assert status !== 'pending' and l1_tx_hash != null within a timeout (suggest 60s given frontend suite already takes 38s). The test should fail with a descriptive message if the lock never advances. Because exact file paths and line numbers are not available, unified_diff is left empty.

fixer_proposals.title: Track future Rust incompatibilities in redis v0.24.0 and sqlx-postgres v0.7.4
fixer_proposals.rationale: Addresses low finding 'Future Rust incompatibilities'. No code change is required now (tests pass, no immediate breakage), but the dependency versions should be pinned with a comment and a tracking issue created. The fix adds a comment block to Cargo.toml noting the known future-incompatibility warnings and the minimum version that resolves them, so the next engineer bumping the Rust toolchain is warned. Because exact line numbers in Cargo.toml are unavailable, unified_diff is left empty.

final_verdict.summary: The Consumer Lock sequence passes all off-chain layers (backend 4/4, frontend 40/40, DB row written with correct pk_dilithium=1952) but fails the L1 anchoring layer because QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY is absent or unset, preventing the lock from ever being submitted to L1Vault. The API silently returns HTTP 200 with status='pending' and never advances the row to 'locked'/'confirmed', giving the consumer a false-positive success signal — a critical silent-failure pattern. The L1 contract and RPC are demonstrably reachable (totalLocked() returned successfully), so this is a configuration/secret injection failure, not an infrastructure outage, and the fix path is well-defined. No fixer proposals were received; remediation requires: (1) injecting QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY into the runtime environment, (2) adding a hard-fail guard in the lock handler so missing signing keys produce a non-200 response or DB error state rather than silent deferral, and (3) extending backend and frontend test assertions to verify l1_tx_hash is non-null and status advances beyond 'pending'.
must_fix_before_merge: Inject QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY secret into CI/runtime environment so L1 transaction signing is possible
Hard-fail lock request (503/500 or rollback) when signing key is unavailable — eliminate silent pending-forever state
Mark DB row with an explicit error state (e.g., status='error', error_msg populated) when L1 submission fails so consumers are not given a false-positive lock_id
Backend seq1_lock test must assert l1_tx_hash IS NOT NULL and status != 'pending' after reasonable timeout
Frontend lock.integration.spec.ts must assert eventual status='locked'/'confirmed', not accept 'pending' as terminal success
unresolved_questions: Is QS__L1_PRIVATE_KEY intentionally omitted in CI (e.g., testnet key not provisioned) or is this an accidental secret injection failure? The answer changes whether the fix is a secret rotation (ops) or a code change (dev).

Does the background L1 anchor task swallow the signing error via Ok(()) / warn-and-continue, or does it never start? The exact code path must be identified to confirm the silent-failure pattern and write a targeted fix.

The L1 layer script queries vault address 0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260 for totalLocked() but the acceptance criteria specify L1 Vault at 0x6F889C00a5e674ab0b9403AfBa0fBEbe30511c67 — are these the same contract under different aliases, or is the monitoring script pointing at the wrong contract?
